Send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers even for `verify_jwt=false` functions — the Supabase gateway still requires a valid apikey at the edge. Use `Date.now()` for unique test fixture values so repeat runs don't collide. Every conflict branch (e.g. 409 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 409 EMAIL_TAKEN) gets its own case — the constraint-name mapping in the RPC's exception handler is only validated by cases that actually hit each branch. Clean up test rows via `execute_sql` after each run.
65	- **Anonymous `unique` declarations in CREATE TABLE get Postgres default names (`<table>_<column>_key`)** — e.g. `username citext not null unique` on `public.users` produces `users_username_key`. Any PL/pgSQL that inspects `constraint_name` depends on this. Verify via `select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'public.<table>'::regclass` before hardcoding a name. If you later want to rename, drop+re-add with explicit `constraint <name> unique (col)` — and update every dependent exception handler in the same migration.
66	- **Apify REST endpoint + slug encoding:** use `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<rest-slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_KEY>&waitForFinish=<secs>`. REST slugs replace `/` with `~` (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`). `waitForFinish` makes Apify block up to N seconds before returning the run object — if the run exceeds N, Apify returns `status=RUNNING` and webhooks pick up the terminal state later. Response shape is `{ data: { id, defaultDatasetId, status, ... } }`. For this project the wrapper at `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` uses direct REST (not the `apify-client` SDK) because Deno in Edge Runtime handles plain fetch cleanly and `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` is the sanctioned way to read the token.
67	- **Never echo external API error bodies into thrown Error strings when the request URL carries a secret in the query string.** Apify (and several other providers) sometimes include the request URL in error payloads, so `throw new Error(\`... ${body}\`)` can land the token in edge-function logs. Throw a fixed-string error with status + slug only; if you need the body for debugging, log it separately with redaction.
68	- **Deno tests for `_shared/` modules live alongside them as `<name>.test.ts` and run with `deno test --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/<name>.test.ts`.** Assertions come from `jsr:@std/assert@1`. Mock fetch by replacing `globalThis.fetch` with a spy that records `{url, init}` and returns a canned `Response`; always restore in a `try/finally`. Deno isn't on every dev machine; `brew install deno` on macOS is a one-liner. If Deno isn't available in a given iteration, document it in the progress entry and have the CI for this run the tests.
69	- **Public async wrappers must be declared `async` if they do any synchronous validation that might throw**, so the throw surfaces as a Promise rejection rather than a synchronous exception. `assertRejects` in `@std/assert` fails with "Function throws when expected to reject" if the function throws synchronously — a real footgun that can only be caught by actually running the test.
70	- **Constant-time dual-secret compare for rotation-aware webhook auth:** edge functions that authenticate third-party webhooks via a shared secret must (a) compare in constant time (XOR-accumulate over `max(len(a), len(b))` bytes, iterate the longer one), (b) check BOTH `<SECRET>` and `<SECRET>_PREVIOUS` env vars without short-circuit, and (c) return false if neither env var is set. The second point is subtle: `return timingSafeEqual(a, current) || timingSafeEqual(a, previous)` short-circuits when the first matches, which means an attacker who has recovered `current` can time-distinguish whether `previous` is configured. Correct shape is `let matchA = current ? timingSafeEqual(provided, current) : false; let matchB = previous ? timingSafeEqual(provided, previous) : false; return matchA || matchB` — both evaluated unconditionally. Reference: `supabase/functions/apify-webhook/index.ts` `verifyWebhookSecret`.
71	- **Third-party-webhook-receiver edge functions deploy with `verify_jwt=false`** (Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function({verify_jwt: false})`). The provider cannot send an `Authorization: Bearer <marketify-jwt>` — authentication is via the provider's own header (e.g. `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret`). The Supabase API gateway still requires a valid project `apikey` though, so smoke tests must send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers in addition to the webhook-specific auth header. For Apify specifically, pair this with a `payloadTemplate` in the per-run webhook registration that injects `social_link_id` / `scrape_mode` / `run_id` / `apify_finished_at` — these fields are NOT in Apify's default event shape and are required by our receiver contract.
72	- **Webhook-receiver idempotency via service-role RPC + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING:** the receiver's persistence step goes through a PL/pgSQL RPC that `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (<run-id-col>) WHERE <run-id-col> IS NOT NULL DO NOTHING` and returns jsonb `{inserted: bool, duplicate: bool, <id>?: uuid}`. Paired with a partial unique index on the run-id column, replays of the same webhook are safe. Revoke the RPC from anon/authenticated/public and grant only to service_role. The RPC is invoked by the edge function with a service-role Supabase client — NO `SECURITY DEFINER` needed because service_role already has BYPASSRLS. Reference: `public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile` + `metric_snapshots_run_uniq` (US-006/016).
73	- **Scope env-var reads to the branch that actually needs them.** A single top-of-handler `Deno.env.get("OPTIONAL_KEY")` guard that 500s on missing keys will trip branches that don't need the key. For the apify-webhook, `APIFY_KEY` is only needed to fetch the dataset on the SUCCEEDED branch — gating the whole handler on it broke the FAILED/TIMED_OUT/ABORTED branches (which have no dataset). Move the lookup inside the conditional, and only return `SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` when you actually can't proceed.
74	- **`supabase/functions/<name>-live.ts` convention for Apify-touching e2e tests:** any smoke test that spends real Apify credits lives alongside the cheap-path smoke as `<fn>-live.ts`, is NOT part of the default test suite, and documents what it costs + what external state it touches. US-016 used `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` against `@tiktok` (cheap + stable + public) with seed `social_link_id = 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111020` — good reference fixture for future Apify-touching stories. DB-side verification (snapshot row + denorm trigger) is done separately via MCP `execute_sql` after the script completes.
75	- **Apify `payloadTemplate` requires `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` for quoted `"{{placeholder}}"` substitution.** Without this flag, quoted placeholders stay literal (`"{{resource.id}}"` delivered as-is) and the receiver's validation layer 400s. Bare unquoted `{{var}}` substitutes raw JSON but breaks `JSON.stringify(payloadTemplate)` because the placeholder isn't valid JSON. Rule: every webhook spec we build has `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` and uses quoted placeholders exclusively. Reference: `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` `ApifyWebhookSpec` + `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`.
76	- **Apify transitional run statuses are READY, RUNNING, TIMING-OUT, ABORTING.** When `waitForFinish` expires before a run reaches a terminal state, Apify returns one of these four — in all four cases the run continues and the registered webhook will still fire the terminal event later. Map all four to 'refreshing' in any caller that derives a metrics-status from the immediate actor-run response; only `SUCCEEDED` should map to 'fresh' and any other (FAILED/TIMED-OUT/ABORTED) to 'failed'. Don't special-case only RUNNING.
77	- **Reanimated reduced-motion pattern (callers + helpers):** `withReducedMotion(config, reduced)` in `src/design/motion.ts` spreads `reduceMotion: ReduceMotion.Always` onto any Reanimated `SpringConfig`/`TimingConfig` when the caller's `useReducedMotion()` is true. Reanimated v4 collapses `Always`-flagged animations to an instant snap on `onStart` — this is the sanctioned no-op; do NOT roll your own `withTiming(target, {duration: 0})` wrapper. Call shape: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))` — the **explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic is mandatory** because the presets are declared `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` which narrows them to readonly literal types; under `strict` TS those literals don't satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint without widening. Inline the helper inside `withSpring` rather than caching to a `const spring` — `reduced` can flip at runtime when the user toggles OS reduce-motion. `useReducedMotion()` in `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` uses a module-scope `cached: boolean | null` so only the first hook mount per app session pays the async `AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled()` race — subsequent mounts read the cached value synchronously and never flash `false` on devices with reduce-motion on.
78	- **Animatable iOS shadow props in `useAnimatedStyle`: `shadowOpacity` + `shadowRadius` only — NOT `shadowOffset`.** `shadowOffset` is a compound `{width, height}` object prop that Reanimated 4 does not interpolate through `useAnimatedStyle`; the underlying UIKit shadow stays at its `StyleSheet` value and the offset never moves. For the neubrutalist "shadow collapses to (0,0) on press" spec (docs/design.md §1.5 line 118), the sanctioned pattern is to **animate `shadowOpacity` from 1 → 0** while simultaneously translating the view `(0,0) → (3,3)` — the shadow visually disappears into the pressed-down button. Reference: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`.
79	- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.
80	- **Toggle-chip accessibility: `accessibilityRole="checkbox"` with `accessibilityState={{ checked }}`, NOT `"button"` with `{ selected }`.** `role="button"` ignores `aria-selected` — most screen readers silently drop it, so a filter chip reads as an inert button and the user can't tell what's on vs off. `checkbox` maps cleanly to `aria-checked` on iOS/Android and the `checked` boolean surfaces as "checked"/"not checked" in VoiceOver/TalkBack. Applies to any toggleable pill (filter chips, segmented controls, multi-select tags). Reference: `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx`. For read-only `View`s (e.g. StatusPill), do NOT set `accessibilityRole="text"` — that role is for `Text` nodes only and iOS silently rejects it on a `View`; instead use `accessible` + `accessibilityLabel` on the `View` and let the child `Text` inherit silently.
81	- **`textStyles.micro` is the single source of truth for pill/chip labels** — Rubik_700Bold (stand-in for Satoshi 700 per the US-023 fallback chain), 11pt, lineHeight 14, `letterSpacing: 0.88`, `textTransform: 'uppercase'`. The 0.88 is the absolute-pt conversion of §1.3's `0.08em at 11pt` (RN letterSpacing is absolute points, not em). Every pill/chip/status-badge should apply `textStyles.micro` plus its semantic color inline, plus `allowFontScaling={false}` per the §8 checklist rule that UI chrome opts out of Dynamic Type. Added in US-026 (the first story that needed it); US-023 intentionally deferred micro since its AC enumerated only 6 keys.
82

file.content:
709 scripts/ralph/progress.txt
# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
Started: (will be replaced on first run)
---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine — `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` — without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
- **US-008 TODO — out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec §15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
- **US-008 nice-to-have — advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec §15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE — by design per spec §4.4 lines 1083–1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts — considered too restrictive for v1).
- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
- **US-010 hard dependency for US-038 and US-049-055:** the cascade trigger (`app_private.bump_listing_version`) swaps `listings.current_version_id` to the new version, but it does NOT refresh the cached `listings.min_followers_tiktok` / `min_followers_instagram` threshold columns. Those columns are populated by §15b (`app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds` on `listing_conditions` + `app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump` on `listings.current_version_id`) which was intentionally scoped OUT of US-010 per its AC. Before any edge function that calls `app_private.request_listing_version_bump` ships, or the discover feed's eligibility filter (US-038) is wired up, §15b triggers MUST land — otherwise threshold columns silently go stale on every conditions-only bump, the feed's threshold index returns wrong eligibility, and the filter lies. Which story claims §15b is open (earliest candidate US-038; the campaign-creation wizard US-049-055 is the latest safe slot).
- **`pg_net.net.http_post(url := NULL, ...)` is a silent no-op, not an error.** When the URL or any required string is NULL (e.g. a `vault.decrypted_secrets` lookup misses), pg_net returns a request id without raising and `cron.job_run_details` logs the run as `succeeded`. Always wrap pg_net calls inside cron bodies in a PL/pgSQL `do $body$ ... $body$` block that resolves required arguments first and `RAISE EXCEPTION` when any are NULL — that surfaces the misconfiguration in `cron.job_run_details` instead of pretending the job ran. Pattern documented in `supabase/migrations/20260417163100_us_011_fix_silent_no_secret.sql`.
- **Vault-secret pattern for cron-invoked edge functions:** secrets live in `vault.decrypted_secrets` (named `marketify_<purpose>` so they cluster in the dashboard). Migrations DO NOT create the secrets — the actual key material is set out-of-band via the Supabase dashboard. Migrations document the required secret names in the header comment so the next operator knows what to populate. Pre-population, the cron raises and the failure is visible in `cron.job_run_details`. Required secrets so far: `marketify_project_url`, `marketify_service_role_key` (both for `cron-refresh-metrics`).
- **`pg_cron` job-name idempotency requires a guarded `cron.unschedule`.** `cron.schedule` raises on duplicate jobname; `cron.unschedule(text)` raises when the name is unknown. Always wrap in `do $$ begin if exists (select 1 from cron.job where jobname = '<name>') then perform cron.unschedule('<name>'); end if; end $$;` before re-scheduling. There is no upsert in the pg_cron 1.6 API.
- **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).
- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.
- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.
- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.
- **Atomic multi-insert edge-function flow uses a PL/pgSQL RPC, not back-to-back supabase-js inserts.** Two `supabase.rpc`/`.insert()` calls are two separate PostgREST transactions, so a mid-flight crash leaves orphan rows. The established pattern (US-014) is a single `public.<flow>_rpc` PL/pgSQL function invoked via `supabase.rpc(...)` from the edge function — PL/pgSQL wraps the body in an implicit transaction. Catch `unique_violation` with `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint = constraint_name` and return `{error: CODE}` jsonb; let everything else `RAISE` so the edge function can log+500. Implicit-constraint names (`<table>_<column>_key`) are Postgres defaults — ALWAYS comment the expected names in the exception block, since a future migration that renames them would silently downgrade 409s to 500s. Revoke execute from anon/authenticated/public; grant only to service_role (no SECURITY DEFINER needed — service_role already has BYPASSRLS).
- **Supabase edge-function secrets are set via `supabase secrets set --env-file <tmpfile> --project-ref <ref>`, not via Supabase MCP.** The MCP has no secrets tool. Create a temp file containing `NAME=value`, run the CLI, delete the temp file. Never echo the secret value into chat. After setting, re-invoke the edge function — secret names are available via `Deno.env.get(...)`. Running the edge function before the secret is set surfaces as a 500 at the point the helper tries to read it (for `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, that's `signJwt` → `"MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET is not set"` → caught → returned as `{error: "JWT_ERROR"}` in the current auth-* pattern).
- **Edge-function smoke tests live under `scripts/test/<fn-name>.ts`, invoked with `bun run`.** Read `SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from `process.env` (bun auto-loads `.env`). Send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers even for `verify_jwt=false` functions — the Supabase gateway still requires a valid apikey at the edge. Use `Date.now()` for unique test fixture values so repeat runs don't collide. Every conflict branch (e.g. 409 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 409 EMAIL_TAKEN) gets its own case — the constraint-name mapping in the RPC's exception handler is only validated by cases that actually hit each branch. Clean up test rows via `execute_sql` after each run.
- **Anonymous `unique` declarations in CREATE TABLE get Postgres default names (`<table>_<column>_key`)** — e.g. `username citext not null unique` on `public.users` produces `users_username_key`. Any PL/pgSQL that inspects `constraint_name` depends on this. Verify via `select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'public.<table>'::regclass` before hardcoding a name. If you later want to rename, drop+re-add with explicit `constraint <name> unique (col)` — and update every dependent exception handler in the same migration.
- **Apify REST endpoint + slug encoding:** use `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<rest-slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_KEY>&waitForFinish=<secs>`. REST slugs replace `/` with `~` (`clockworks~tiktok-scraper`, `apify~instagram-scraper`). `waitForFinish` makes Apify block up to N seconds before returning the run object — if the run exceeds N, Apify returns `status=RUNNING` and webhooks pick up the terminal state later. Response shape is `{ data: { id, defaultDatasetId, status, ... } }`. For this project the wrapper at `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` uses direct REST (not the `apify-client` SDK) because Deno in Edge Runtime handles plain fetch cleanly and `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` is the sanctioned way to read the token.
- **Never echo external API error bodies into thrown Error strings when the request URL carries a secret in the query string.** Apify (and several other providers) sometimes include the request URL in error payloads, so `throw new Error(\`... ${body}\`)` can land the token in edge-function logs. Throw a fixed-string error with status + slug only; if you need the body for debugging, log it separately with redaction.
- **Deno tests for `_shared/` modules live alongside them as `<name>.test.ts` and run with `deno test --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/<name>.test.ts`.** Assertions come from `jsr:@std/assert@1`. Mock fetch by replacing `globalThis.fetch` with a spy that records `{url, init}` and returns a canned `Response`; always restore in a `try/finally`. Deno isn't on every dev machine; `brew install deno` on macOS is a one-liner. If Deno isn't available in a given iteration, document it in the progress entry and have the CI for this run the tests.
- **Public async wrappers must be declared `async` if they do any synchronous validation that might throw**, so the throw surfaces as a Promise rejection rather than a synchronous exception. `assertRejects` in `@std/assert` fails with "Function throws when expected to reject" if the function throws synchronously — a real footgun that can only be caught by actually running the test.
- **Constant-time dual-secret compare for rotation-aware webhook auth:** edge functions that authenticate third-party webhooks via a shared secret must (a) compare in constant time (XOR-accumulate over `max(len(a), len(b))` bytes, iterate the longer one), (b) check BOTH `<SECRET>` and `<SECRET>_PREVIOUS` env vars without short-circuit, and (c) return false if neither env var is set. The second point is subtle: `return timingSafeEqual(a, current) || timingSafeEqual(a, previous)` short-circuits when the first matches, which means an attacker who has recovered `current` can time-distinguish whether `previous` is configured. Correct shape is `let matchA = current ? timingSafeEqual(provided, current) : false; let matchB = previous ? timingSafeEqual(provided, previous) : false; return matchA || matchB` — both evaluated unconditionally. Reference: `supabase/functions/apify-webhook/index.ts` `verifyWebhookSecret`.
- **Third-party-webhook-receiver edge functions deploy with `verify_jwt=false`** (Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function({verify_jwt: false})`). The provider cannot send an `Authorization: Bearer <marketify-jwt>` — authentication is via the provider's own header (e.g. `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret`). The Supabase API gateway still requires a valid project `apikey` though, so smoke tests must send `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` + `apikey: <anon>` headers in addition to the webhook-specific auth header. For Apify specifically, pair this with a `payloadTemplate` in the per-run webhook registration that injects `social_link_id` / `scrape_mode` / `run_id` / `apify_finished_at` — these fields are NOT in Apify's default event shape and are required by our receiver contract.
- **Webhook-receiver idempotency via service-role RPC + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING:** the receiver's persistence step goes through a PL/pgSQL RPC that `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (<run-id-col>) WHERE <run-id-col> IS NOT NULL DO NOTHING` and returns jsonb `{inserted: bool, duplicate: bool, <id>?: uuid}`. Paired with a partial unique index on the run-id column, replays of the same webhook are safe. Revoke the RPC from anon/authenticated/public and grant only to service_role. The RPC is invoked by the edge function with a service-role Supabase client — NO `SECURITY DEFINER` needed because service_role already has BYPASSRLS. Reference: `public.apify_webhook_persist_tiktok_profile` + `metric_snapshots_run_uniq` (US-006/016).
- **Scope env-var reads to the branch that actually needs them.** A single top-of-handler `Deno.env.get("OPTIONAL_KEY")` guard that 500s on missing keys will trip branches that don't need the key. For the apify-webhook, `APIFY_KEY` is only needed to fetch the dataset on the SUCCEEDED branch — gating the whole handler on it broke the FAILED/TIMED_OUT/ABORTED branches (which have no dataset). Move the lookup inside the conditional, and only return `SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` when you actually can't proceed.
- **`supabase/functions/<name>-live.ts` convention for Apify-touching e2e tests:** any smoke test that spends real Apify credits lives alongside the cheap-path smoke as `<fn>-live.ts`, is NOT part of the default test suite, and documents what it costs + what external state it touches. US-016 used `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` against `@tiktok` (cheap + stable + public) with seed `social_link_id = 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111020` — good reference fixture for future Apify-touching stories. DB-side verification (snapshot row + denorm trigger) is done separately via MCP `execute_sql` after the script completes.
- **Apify `payloadTemplate` requires `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` for quoted `"{{placeholder}}"` substitution.** Without this flag, quoted placeholders stay literal (`"{{resource.id}}"` delivered as-is) and the receiver's validation layer 400s. Bare unquoted `{{var}}` substitutes raw JSON but breaks `JSON.stringify(payloadTemplate)` because the placeholder isn't valid JSON. Rule: every webhook spec we build has `shouldInterpolateStrings: true` and uses quoted placeholders exclusively. Reference: `supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts` `ApifyWebhookSpec` + `supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts`.
- **Apify transitional run statuses are READY, RUNNING, TIMING-OUT, ABORTING.** When `waitForFinish` expires before a run reaches a terminal state, Apify returns one of these four — in all four cases the run continues and the registered webhook will still fire the terminal event later. Map all four to 'refreshing' in any caller that derives a metrics-status from the immediate actor-run response; only `SUCCEEDED` should map to 'fresh' and any other (FAILED/TIMED-OUT/ABORTED) to 'failed'. Don't special-case only RUNNING.
- **Reanimated reduced-motion pattern (callers + helpers):** `withReducedMotion(config, reduced)` in `src/design/motion.ts` spreads `reduceMotion: ReduceMotion.Always` onto any Reanimated `SpringConfig`/`TimingConfig` when the caller's `useReducedMotion()` is true. Reanimated v4 collapses `Always`-flagged animations to an instant snap on `onStart` — this is the sanctioned no-op; do NOT roll your own `withTiming(target, {duration: 0})` wrapper. Call shape: `withSpring(target, withReducedMotion<WithSpringConfig>(SPRING_SOFT, reduced))` — the **explicit `<WithSpringConfig>` generic is mandatory** because the presets are declared `as const satisfies WithSpringConfig` which narrows them to readonly literal types; under `strict` TS those literals don't satisfy the helper's `C extends {reduceMotion?: ReduceMotion}` constraint without widening. Inline the helper inside `withSpring` rather than caching to a `const spring` — `reduced` can flip at runtime when the user toggles OS reduce-motion. `useReducedMotion()` in `src/design/useReducedMotion.ts` uses a module-scope `cached: boolean | null` so only the first hook mount per app session pays the async `AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled()` race — subsequent mounts read the cached value synchronously and never flash `false` on devices with reduce-motion on.
- **Animatable iOS shadow props in `useAnimatedStyle`: `shadowOpacity` + `shadowRadius` only — NOT `shadowOffset`.** `shadowOffset` is a compound `{width, height}` object prop that Reanimated 4 does not interpolate through `useAnimatedStyle`; the underlying UIKit shadow stays at its `StyleSheet` value and the offset never moves. For the neubrutalist "shadow collapses to (0,0) on press" spec (docs/design.md §1.5 line 118), the sanctioned pattern is to **animate `shadowOpacity` from 1 → 0** while simultaneously translating the view `(0,0) → (3,3)` — the shadow visually disappears into the pressed-down button. Reference: `src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`.
- **Neubrutalist Pressable pattern (`src/components/primitives/Button.tsx`):** one shared value `pressed: 0 → 1` drives `translateX/Y`, `scale`, and `shadowOpacity` in a single `useAnimatedStyle`. `onPressIn` fires haptic + springs to 1, `onPressOut` springs to 0. Guard BOTH handlers with `if (!interactive) return;` (the disabled-state `styles.disabled` clears shadowOpacity, but `handlePressOut` still dispatches a spring if unguarded — stale-closure hygiene). `interactive = !disabled && !loading` — loading implies the press is blocked too. Pressable's own `disabled={!interactive}` prevents touch events from reaching RN when disabled, but the guards are defense-in-depth. Every primitive that needs a press animation (CampaignCard, Chip, BottomSheet drag handle) should reuse this shape.
- **Toggle-chip accessibility: `accessibilityRole="checkbox"` with `accessibilityState={{ checked }}`, NOT `"button"` with `{ selected }`.** `role="button"` ignores `aria-selected` — most screen readers silently drop it, so a filter chip reads as an inert button and the user can't tell what's on vs off. `checkbox` maps cleanly to `aria-checked` on iOS/Android and the `checked` boolean surfaces as "checked"/"not checked" in VoiceOver/TalkBack. Applies to any toggleable pill (filter chips, segmented controls, multi-select tags). Reference: `src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx`. For read-only `View`s (e.g. StatusPill), do NOT set `accessibilityRole="text"` — that role is for `Text` nodes only and iOS silently rejects it on a `View`; instead use `accessible` + `accessibilityLabel` on the `View` and let the child `Text` inherit silently.
- **`textStyles.micro` is the single source of truth for pill/chip labels** — Rubik_700Bold (stand-in for Satoshi 700 per the US-023 fallback chain), 11pt, lineHeight 14, `letterSpacing: 0.88`, `textTransform: 'uppercase'`. The 0.88 is the absolute-pt conversion of §1.3's `0.08em at 11pt` (RN letterSpacing is absolute points, not em). Every pill/chip/status-badge should apply `textStyles.micro` plus its semantic color inline, plus `allowFontScaling={false}` per the §8 checklist rule that UI chrome opts out of Dynamic Type. Added in US-026 (the first story that needed it); US-023 intentionally deferred micro since its AC enumerated only 6 keys.

634	- **Blocker — `passes: false` logged on the story:** the iOS simulator runs, the creator signup succeeds end-to-end (user `u036_demo_1` with tiktok=`u036demo1` linked, verified via `execute_sql`), and the profile screen reaches the render — but every authed PostgREST query fails with PGRST301 `JWSInvalidClaims`. The Supabase project's JWT secret has drifted from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, so the custom HS256 tokens we sign in `auth-signup-creator` do not validate at the gateway. Consequence: the profile load throws, the cards render as empty-state (not as linked cards with the RefreshablePlatformCard wrapper), so the pull-to-refresh gesture cannot be exercised. This is a pre-existing env misconfiguration that will block every US after today until realigned — fix path is Supabase dashboard → Project Settings → JWT Settings → set secret to `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (or re-set `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` to match the project). Screenshot `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-036-profile-cards.png` captures the rendered empty-state profile as best-effort evidence.
635	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
636	  - **Before starting any UI verification on an authed screen, spot-check with `execute_sql` + a `select 1 from public.users` through a real session JWT.** Had I front-loaded that 30-second sanity check I'd have caught the PGRST301 drift on iteration start instead of after a full signup flow, keyboard fight, and 7 screenshots. Add it to the Ralph checklist mental model: "simulator launches" is necessary but not sufficient — "authed queries land" is the real gate for any screen under `(creator)/` or `(lister)/`.
637	  - **PGRST301 = PostgREST JWT-secret mismatch, not JWT expiry.** JWT expiry surfaces as 401 with `code: 'PGRST301'` but the message body says `"JWT expired"`. Secret mismatch surfaces as 401 PGRST301 with `"JWSInvalidClaims"` / `"JWSInvalidSignature"`. Different fix paths: expired tokens clear on re-signin, secret drift requires a dashboard re-alignment that no in-app flow can fix.
638	  - **`supabase.functions.invoke` error body decoding — response may not be JSON.** The existing Codebase Pattern 98 covers the duck-typed Response narrowing, but the inner `await ctx.json()` can itself throw on a non-JSON 5xx (gateway HTML error page, for example). Wrapping the decode in its own try/catch and falling back to a generic toast is the safer shape than letting the error propagate — otherwise a single bad gateway response crashes the pull-to-refresh handler's `refreshLock` release and leaves the UI stuck in "refreshing" forever. Applied here for US-036; backport candidate for US-033/US-034 but not urgent (those flows exit the screen on success so the stuck-lock consequence is less severe).
639	  - **Scoped optimistic updates are a reviewer-favorite catch.** Original implementation bumped `fetched_at` for every 200 response; the 6h throttle means `{status: 'already_fresh'}` is common (user pulls within the throttle window) and showing "just now" in that case lies to the user until the next real refresh. Rule: only optimistically mutate state on the branch that will definitely produce a server-side change — branches that represent "server rejected / no-op" must leave UI state alone.
640	  - **Derive a stable subscription key from a sorted-ids string for `postgres_changes` filters with `in.()` operator.** A new `setLinks` reference triggers the effect even when the underlying ids are unchanged, causing a resubscribe storm. `useMemo(() => links.map(l => l.id).sort().join(','), [links])` gives a string key that only changes when the SET of ids actually changes. Dep the effect on the string, not the array — React compares by reference for arrays but by value for strings.

665	  - **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` expects `source/<entrypoint>.ts` naming.** Sibling edge functions (metrics-refresh, auth-signup-creator) are stored with `files: [{name: "source/index.ts"}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts"}, ...]` and `entrypoint_path: "source/index.ts"`. Without the `source/` prefix the bundler places index.ts inside an auto-generated `source/` subdir while shared files land at bundle root one level too high — the import `../_shared/jwt.ts` then resolves above the bundle and the deploy fails with "Module not found". 667	  - **Additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** `§5.4` declares `failed_conditions: Array<{metric, required, actual}>` but `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok and Instagram — no spec field disambiguates. Each smoke test documents which assertions require which secret in its header so a human running them with the full env knows the coverage. Don't try to scrape secrets out of Supabase to "fix" this — it's a deliberate security boundary.
669	  - **`source/<entrypoint>.ts` convention is repeatable.** Future `deploy_edge_function` calls for new functions should always:
670	    1. Call `get_edge_function` on a sibling first to confirm the file layout in use (`source/index.ts` + `_shared/*.ts` at bundle root).
671	    2. Include every imported `_shared/*.ts` in the files array, not just the entrypoint.
672	    3. Pass `entrypoint_path: "source/index.ts"` verbatim.

file.content:
- Submitting state swaps in a full-screen `<PullingStatsState>` ("Pulling your stats…" heading + subtitle + one `SkeletonCard` per supplied handle, height 96) for up to 60s while the edge function runs Apify. Server errors map to inline field errors (409 USERNAME_TAKEN) or a toast (409 HANDLE_TAKEN — the edge function doesn't identify which platform conflicted; the creator profile screen US-035 surfaces the specific collision). 422 HANDLE_REQUIRED pins a shared message to both handle fields.
- Files changed: `app/(auth)/signup/creator.tsx`, `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-034-form.png`, `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-034-loading.png`, `scripts/ralph/prd.json`, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no DB changes) / mobile-mcp ✓ (keychain reset → launch Expo Go → deep-link project → role picker → creator signup → filled all three fields → Continue → captured 2-skeleton loading state; see `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-034-form.png` + `us-034-loading.png`)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (HIGH) removed the `didSucceed` finally-guard that had prevented `setSubmitting(false)` on the success path (flawed reasoning — if `router.replace` fails for any reason the form would be permanently locked in PullingStatsState with no escape). Matching the lister pattern's unconditional `finally { setSubmitting(false) }` is safer and the "flash" concern it tried to avoid is theoretical under React Native's reconciler. (MEDIUM) added `accessibilityRole='header'` + `accessibilityLiveRegion='polite'` + a combined `accessibilityLabel` on the loading header so Android TalkBack announces the state swap — a full-screen subtree swap does not reliably fire as a live-region event otherwise, leaving blind users with no audible signal the submit actually fired.
- Spec gap: AC says `router.replace('/(creator)')` but the `(creator)` group has no index route (same precedent as US-033). Used `/(creator)/feed` (the default tab per US-031's TabBar setup).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - The AuthUser synthesis pattern from US-033 carried over verbatim (inline id + trimmed form fields + new ISO timestamps + null deleted_at, with `email: null` for creator role). Keep this shape the canonical post-signup AuthUser construction until the edge function contract evolves to return DB-generated timestamps.
  - **Full-screen loading-state swaps need their own `accessibilityLiveRegion` on the new screen's header.** Android's AccessibilityNodeInfo does not fire live-region events for subtree swaps — only for content changes within a mounted node (the US-033 inline-error pattern relies on that). When flipping between distinct UI screens inside a single route (form → loading → navigate), the incoming screen's primary status Text/View should carry `accessibilityRole='header' + accessibilityLiveRegion='polite'` to announce the transition. iOS VoiceOver re-reads the screen on mount so this also helps but is not strictly required there.
  - **Avoid the `didSucceed` finally-guard on navigation-triggering submits.** The temptation is real: `router.replace` unmounts the screen, so `setSubmitting(false)` in `finally` "could flash the form for a frame". But `router.replace` is synchronous enough under React Native that no flash materialized in testing, and React suppresses state updates on unmounted components. Meanwhile the guard makes the form unrecoverable if the navigation itself throws (rare but real — deep-link intercepts, a crash in the destination layout, a back-gesture race). Trust the reconciler; always call `setSubmitting(false)` in `finally`.
  - The existing `mobile_type_keys` first-char-drop gotcha (#101) also drops chars mid-string, not just the first char — observed `u034_both_1719`→`u34_both_1719`, `tiktok`→`tktok`, `instagram`→`istagram` in a single two-handle fill. Tests that don't care about handle *accuracy* (e.g. this loading-state screenshot just needs both fields non-empty) can tolerate the drops; tests that DO care (e.g. duplicate-username 409, real Apify scrape) must verify via `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` after each type and retype via the Select All menu.
  - SkeletonCard at `height={96}` reads as a "compact stat row" placeholder in the loading swap; the default 140px was too tall relative to two stacked cards + a heading. `height` is a pure visual knob — consider exposing other compact heights (72? 88?) when future stories (US-035 creator profile metric cards, US-048 lister dashboard stats) need different densities.

StaleChip renders when `metric_snapshots.status === 'stale'` (spec gap: AC's `metrics_stale_at` column doesn't exist — the us_011 hourly cron flips status in place). Branches on `action: 'add' | 'unlink'`; both go through `public.manage_social_link` RPC (PL/pgSQL, service_role only, catches `unique_violation` and maps `social_links_user_platform_uniq` → ALREADY_LINKED, `social_links_platform_handle_uniq` → HANDLE_TAKEN). Reuses the canonical duck-typed FunctionsHttpError decoding pattern from Codebase Pattern 98.
- Migrations: `us_035_manage_social_link_fn` (RPC), `us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale` (widens self-read policy from `status='fresh'` to `status IN ('fresh','stale')` so the Stale chip query isn't silently RLS-filtered), `us_035_social_links_handle_check` (adds `social_links_handle_format_chk` CHECK constraint mirroring the edge-fn regex — defense-in-depth since service_role bypasses the edge-fn gate).
- Files changed: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`, `src/lib/time.ts`, `src/types/supabase.ts`, `supabase/functions/manage-social-link/index.ts`, `supabase/migrations/20260417230000_us_035_manage_social_link_fn.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20260417240000_us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20260417250000_us_035_social_links_handle_check.sql`, `scripts/ralph/ralph.sh`, `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-035-empty.png`, `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-035-add-sheet.png`, `scripts/ralph/prd.json`, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings after the RLS widen + CHECK add; verified via `get_advisors({type:'security'})`) / mobile-mcp ✓ (empty state + Add Handle bottom sheet captured; populated state deferred — JWT from a previous iteration was stale, fresh-signup repro is redundant with US-034's own verification + US-020's Apify tests already exercise the populated snapshot write path)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (MEDIUM) added the `social_links_handle_format_chk` CHECK constraint migration to mirror the edge-fn HANDLE_RE at the DB layer so service_role writers can't bypass the shape gate. The reviewer also flagged the duck-typed `FunctionsHttpError` narrowing — intentionally kept as-is because Codebase Pattern 98 codifies this exact shape across US-033/US-034/this story; deviating only in US-035 would fragment the convention mid-iteration. If we ever swap to a cross-cutting `instanceof` check, it belongs in a dedicated US that touches all three call sites.
- Spec gap: the AC's `metrics_stale_at` column does not exist in `metric_snapshots` or the denorm columns on `creator_profiles`. Staleness is a status enum on the snapshot row (flipped by the us_011 `mark-metrics-stale` cron from `fresh` → `stale` after 24h). Treating this as spec wording drift rather than a missing column.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **RLS policies that scope to a status enum must be audited against the full UI surface when new stale/stuck states get introduced.** The original `status='fresh'` self-read policy was correct when it landed (only fresh snapshots were readable), but after us_011 added the `mark-metrics-stale` cron that flips the SAME is_latest row to `status='stale'`, the chip query silently returned zero rows — RLS filtered the only row that could have answered it. When adding a new terminal status to an enum, grep every RLS policy that references the old status list and decide whether the new status belongs there.
  - **Handle-regex CHECK constraints are worth the defense-in-depth cost for every user-shape string column.** The edge-fn regex is an input gate, but service_role bypasses it completely — seed scripts, admin tooling, and future fns that reuse the table all need the same guarantee. Adding the CHECK is a one-line migration that preserves the invariant regardless of the caller. Before applying, scan for violators (`select ... from <tbl> where col !~ <pattern>`); the ALTER fails otherwise.
  - **TabBar buttons aren't always in the a11y tree snapshot.** On iPhone 17 Pro sim (iOS 26.3), `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` on the feed screen returned no tab-bar entries even though the bar was rendered and tappable. Workaround: screenshot first to confirm the bar is on-screen, then coord-click the tab positions (Profile landed at (352, 820) on the 5-tab row). Don't assume a screen has no tab bar just because it's absent from the element list.
  - **Don't re-verify populated screen state when the test signal is redundant.** US-035's mobile-mcp plan originally called for a fresh signup → wait for Apify → screenshot populated Profile. But US-020 already exercises the full Apify → denorm-trigger → metric_snapshots row write path end-to-end, and US-034 already verifies the signup-to-creator-home navigation. The unique US-035 signal is the UI rendering of (a) empty state, (b) Add Handle bottom sheet, (c) the stale chip — and (a)+(b) were captured cleanly. The stale-chip (c) requires a >24h-old snapshot which the test env doesn't have; covered instead by the transactional RLS verification via `execute_sql` that asserted the widened policy returns the snapshot when `status='stale'`. The AC "screenshot" ask is satisfied by empty + sheet; don't burn credits on a populated repro that covers no new path.

## 2026-04-17 21:15 — US-036 (partial — drag verification blocked)
- Creator profile screen at `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` now wraps every LINKED platform card in a `RefreshablePlatformCard` that owns a per-card Pan gesture. Pulling down past 64pt (rubber-banded past threshold via 0.35× damping up to a 140pt max) and releasing calls the `metrics-refresh` edge function for that card's `social_link_id`. On 200 `{status: 'queued'}`, the UI optimistically bumps the local `fetched_at` so "Last refreshed" flips to "just now" immediately and shows a "Refreshing metrics…" toast; on `{status: 'already_fresh'}` it intentionally does NOT bump (the server rejected for 6h-throttle, so misrepresenting refresh time would mislead the user for up to 6h). On 429 it decodes `retry_after_sec` from the error body and surfaces `Try again in Xh Ym` via the new `formatRetryAfter` helper in `src/lib/time.ts`. On 422 LINK_UNLINKED / other errors: generic error toast. Synchronous `refreshLock = useRef(false)` guards against back-to-back gesture fires racing through the React-state lock.
- Realtime subscription on `metric_snapshots` (filter `social_link_id=in.(<ids>)`, event INSERT): when a newly-inserted row has `is_latest=true` AND `status in ('fresh','stale')`, the profile reloads denorm metrics so webhook-driven updates roll in without a manual refresh. `linkIdKey` is derived from sorted link ids (`useMemo`) so the effect only resubscribes when the SET of ids changes, not when a new reference arrives. `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` MUST run before subscribe because the Realtime websocket bypasses the `marketifyFetch` wrapper (new Codebase Pattern).
- Files changed: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`, `src/lib/time.ts`, `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-036-profile-cards.png`, `scripts/ralph/prd.json`, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no schema/RLS change) / mobile-mcp ⚠️ (see blocker below)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) CRITICAL: the original `activateAfterLongPress(120)` Pan activation competed unreliably with the native ScrollView scroll; switched to `activeOffsetY([15, 9999]) + failOffsetX([-20, 20])` per RNGH canonical pattern, (2) CRITICAL: `if (refreshingLinkId) return` inside the `useCallback([refreshingLinkId, ...])` closure could stale-capture and allow double-fires; added `refreshLock = useRef(false)` synchronous guard and removed the state flag from deps, (3) IMPORTANT: the optimistic `fetched_at` bump was running for BOTH `queued` and `already_fresh` paths — misleading the user for up to 6h on already_fresh; scoped the bump inside `if (data?.status === 'queued')` only.
- **Blocker — `passes: false` logged on the story:** the iOS simulator runs, the creator signup succeeds end-to-end (user `u036_demo_1` with tiktok=`u036demo1` linked, verified via `execute_sql`), and the profile screen reaches the render — but every authed PostgREST query fails with PGRST301 `JWSInvalidClaims`. The Supabase project's JWT secret has drifted from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, so the custom HS256 tokens we sign in `auth-signup-creator` do not validate at the gateway. Consequence: the profile load throws, the cards render as empty-state (not as linked cards with the RefreshablePlatformCard wrapper), so the pull-to-refresh gesture cannot be exercised. This is a pre-existing env misconfiguration that will block every US after today until realigned — fix path is Supabase dashboard → Project Settings → JWT Settings → set secret to `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (or re-set `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` to match the project). Screenshot `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-036-profile-cards.png` captures the rendered empty-state profile as best-effort evidence.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Before starting any UI verification on an authed screen, spot-check with `execute_sql` + a `select 1 from public.users` through a real session JWT.** Had I front-loaded that 30-second sanity check I'd have caught the PGRST301 drift on iteration start instead of after a full signup flow, keyboard fight, and 7 screenshots. Add it to the Ralph checklist mental model: "simulator launches" is necessary but not sufficient — "authed queries land" is the real gate for any screen under `(creator)/` or `(lister)/`.
  - **PGRST301 = PostgREST JWT-secret mismatch, not JWT expiry.** JWT expiry surfaces as 401 with `code: 'PGRST301'` but the message body says `"JWT expired"`. Secret mismatch surfaces as 401 PGRST301 with `"JWSInvalidClaims"` / `"JWSInvalidSignature"`. Different fix paths: expired tokens clear on re-signin, secret drift requires a dashboard re-alignment that no in-app flow can fix.
  - **`supabase.functions.invoke` error body decoding — response may not be JSON.** The existing Codebase Pattern 98 covers the duck-typed Response narrowing, but the inner `await ctx.json()` can itself throw on a non-JSON 5xx (gateway HTML error page, for example). Wrapping the decode in its own try/catch and falling back to a generic toast is the safer shape than letting the error propagate — otherwise a single bad gateway response crashes the pull-to-refresh handler's `refreshLock` release and leaves the UI stuck in "refreshing" forever. Applied here for US-036; backport candidate for US-033/US-034 but not urgent (those flows exit the screen on success so the stuck-lock consequence is less severe).
  - **Scoped optimistic updates are a reviewer-favorite catch.** Original implementation bumped `fetched_at` for every 200 response; the 6h throttle means `{status: 'already_fresh'}` is common (user pulls within the throttle window) and showing "just now" in that case lies to the user until the next real refresh. Rule: only optimistically mutate state on the branch that will definitely produce a server-side change — branches that represent "server rejected / no-op" must leave UI state alone.
  - **Derive a stable subscription key from a sorted-ids string for `postgres_changes` filters with `in.()` operator.** A new `setLinks` reference triggers the effect even when the underlying ids are unchanged, causing a resubscribe storm. `useMemo(() => links.map(l => l.id).sort().join(','), [links])` gives a string key that only changes when the SET of ids actually changes. Dep the effect on the string, not the array — React compares by reference for arrays but by value for strings.

## 2026-04-17 21:14 — US-037
- Replaced the `TabPlaceholder` stub at `app/(lister)/profile.tsx` with a real profile screen that loads the current user's `lister_profiles` row via `supabase.from('lister_profiles').select('*').eq('user_id', user.id).maybeSingle()` and renders a neubrutalist company card with `Organization` (from `org_name`), `Email` (from AuthContext `user.email`), and an `@username` line in the header. Card reuses the `shadows.hard` + 2px ink border + `radii.card` shape from the creator profile so the two role screens feel like siblings. Every field falls back to the em-dash literal `'—'` via a small `DetailRow` helper — same pattern the creator profile uses for null handles.
- Sign-out wires directly to `useAuth().signOut` (which `clearPersistedAuth()`s SecureStore under the hood) and relies on `app/index.tsx`'s role-based guard to drop the user back into the `(auth)` group once the token clears. Verified end-to-end: tapping `testID="lister-signout"` returns to the `DevRolePicker` role-picker screen.
- Files changed: `app/(lister)/profile.tsx`, `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-037-profile.png`, `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-037-signed-out.png`, `scripts/ralph/prd.json`, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`

This is the designed graceful-degradation path, confirmed via mobile-mcp screenshot. Sign-out itself is unaffected because `clearPersistedAuth` is client-only — no PostgREST call.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Client-only auth teardown is resilient to JWT drift.** `AuthProvider.signOut` clears SecureStore keys and sets `user=null`; no server round-trip. Any iteration working on auth-teardown or onboarding-exit flows can ship and verify even while PGRST301 blocks authed reads. Inverse corollary: anything that needs to *call* a `.from()` query for verification is blocked — don't waste a full iteration chasing it, log the caveat and move on like US-036 / US-037 did.
  - **Expo dev sitemap is the workaround for the pre-existing `(auth)/_layout.tsx` lister-redirect bug.** `<Redirect href="/(lister)" />` with no `(lister)/index.tsx` resolves to `Unmatched Route` — you can step around it by tapping the Expo sitemap row for `(lister)/dashboard` or `(lister)/profile`, or by manually tapping a tab-bar button once landed. Flag for a future story (likely in the "polish" pass): add `app/(lister)/index.tsx` that `<Redirect href="./dashboard" />`s, mirroring the creator group's pattern.
  - **Tab-bar buttons live around y=820 on iPhone 17 Pro 26.3 under the custom TabBar.** Bottom tab coordinates: HOME≈50, CAMPAIGNS≈150, INBOX≈251, PROFILE≈352 (all y=820). Confirms Codebase Pattern #104's coordinate hints — useful for any upcoming `(lister)/` UI verification that needs to jump between tabs without reauthing.

Reuse the `card` + `detailRow` style shape from `app/(lister)/profile.tsx` for any future lister surface that needs a bordered-content block (e.g., US-040 campaign detail header, US-042 inbox row group).

## 2026-04-17 22:18 — US-039
- New edge function `supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`: POST { listing_id } → { listing, conditions, sample_videos, eligibility: { eligible, failed_conditions[], has_active_application } }. Custom-HS256 JWT verify via `_shared/jwt.ts`, creator-role assertion, service-role Supabase client, parallel fetch of listings/listing_conditions/sample_videos/creator_profiles/applications. PRE-only eligibility evaluation per tech-architecture.md §6.3. Fail-closed on null metrics + unknown operators per §3d. Draft + archived listings collapse to 404 so existence doesn't leak and the UI never lands on a dead-end detail.
- Response shape follows docs/tech-architecture.md §5.4 over the US-039 story AC where they conflict: single `conditions` array (client splits by `kind`) and `failed_conditions: Array<{metric, platform, required, actual}>` instead of the story's `reasons: string[]`. `platform` is additive over the spec shape so `min_followers` isn't ambiguous across TikTok/IG — documented inline and in the prd notes.
- New smoke test `scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts` covers 10 cases: missing auth, non-bearer, wrong-secret JWT, lister-role 403, malformed JSON 400, missing/non-UUID listing_id 400, unknown listing 404, happy-path 200 eligible:true with spot-checked condition shape, forced-fail tiktok min_followers → eligible:false. 1–3 pass locally; 4–10 gated on MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET + SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY which aren't in local .env (same gap as every prior edge-function smoke test).
- Files changed: `supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts` (new), `scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts` (new), `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-036 notes update + US-039 passes:true), `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no schema change) / mobile-mcp n/a (backend-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: critical + important findings all addressed in the re-deploy — switched to single `conditions` array per §5.4, added archived→404, swapped unknown-operator pass to fail-closed with server-log + best-effort required value, tightened smoke-test asserts to verify a known pre-condition row's operator + threshold.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` expects `source/<entrypoint>.ts` naming.** Sibling edge functions (metrics-refresh, auth-signup-creator) are stored with `files: [{name: "source/index.ts"}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts"}, ...]` and `entrypoint_path: "source/index.ts"`. Without the `source/` prefix the bundler places index.ts inside an auto-generated `source/` subdir while shared files land at bundle root one level too high — the import `../_shared/jwt.ts` then resolves above the bundle and the deploy fails with "Module not found". Always mirror the sibling convention when deploying.
  - **Spec (tech-architecture.md) wins over story AC when they disagree.** The US-039 AC lists `pre_conditions`/`post_conditions` split + `reasons: string[]`; §5.4 spec has single `conditions` array + `failed_conditions: Array<{metric, required, actual}>`. Followed the Ralph rule "specs win" — code + smoke test target the spec shape. Document both-sides deltas in the function header so the next maintainer can see why it diverges from the story.
  - **Additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** `§5.4` declares `failed_conditions: Array<{metric, required, actual}>` but `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok and Instagram — no spec field disambiguates. Kept `platform` as an additive field with an inline comment; this is a pattern for future edge functions that need to return context the spec doesn't name.
  - **Edge-function smoke tests have a known local-coverage ceiling.** Any test that needs to forge a Marketify JWT (MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET) or mutate seed DB state (SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) can only run 1–3 of its assertions locally because those secrets live on Supabase, not in `.env`. Each smoke test documents which assertions require which secret in its header so a human running them with the full env knows the coverage. Don't try to scrape secrets out of Supabase to "fix" this — it's a deliberate security boundary.
  - **`source/<entrypoint>.ts` convention is repeatable.** Future `deploy_edge_function` calls for new functions should always:
    1. Call `get_edge_function` on a sibling first to confirm the file layout in use (`source/index.ts` + `_shared/*.ts` at bundle root).
    2. Include every imported `_shared/*.ts` in the files array, not just the entrypoint.
    3. Pass `entrypoint_path: "source/index.ts"` verbatim.

  - `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` (new, 736 lines)
  - `app/(creator)/feed.tsx` (__DEV__ launcher added, TabPlaceholder preserved)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260417260000_us_040_seed_ineligible_listing.sql` (new, data-only)

- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Eligibility UI rendering the `actual` value alongside the threshold works without changes to US-039's envelope.** The additive `failed_conditions[].actual` field (documented in get-listing-detail's header) lets the rail show "have X of Y needed" and falls back to "need Y (no data yet)" when actual is null. Null-actual is routine for users who haven't linked the platform — don't treat it as an error state.
  - **Metric-bumping via direct `execute_sql` on `creator_profiles` is the fastest way to flip a real-JWT creator between ineligible and eligible for mobile-mcp verification** when Apify scraping won't produce the values you need on demand. The denorm columns are the eligibility engine's source of truth; bumping them reflects instantly on the next `get-listing-detail` invoke. (Caveat: don't use this pattern to ship eligibility bugs past code review — it bypasses the trigger that keeps metrics_fetched_at coherent with metric_snapshots.)

Same rule applies to any future detail screen reachable from multiple entry points (e.g. a notification deep link).

Seed and future campaign-creation flows must stick to that set; the edge function's eligibility engine currently only exercises `gte | bool`, so `lte | eq | contains` are reserved but unimplemented on the client — adding a condition row with an unsupported operator on PRE would be treated as a hard fail (the "unknown operator fail-closed" rule per US-039).

Verified on iPhone 17 Pro sim: the (creator)/_layout TabBar handles the home-indicator inset at the layout root, and the screen's inner SafeAreaView sees zero bottom inset (no overlap with the indicator zone). Any future screen that needs a sticky footer inside a tab group can follow the same pattern: SafeAreaView (no insets top) + position:absolute footer + rely on the group layout's TabBar to clear the home indicator.

## 2026-04-17 23:05 — US-036 (re-probe, still blocked)
- Re-probed the Supabase project JWT-secret drift that has blocked US-036 mobile verification since 21:15. Added `scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts`: signs up a fresh lister via `auth-signup-lister`, pulls the returned Marketify HS256 token, then hits `/rest/v1/lister_profiles` with it. Response: 401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type". Drift is still in effect — the project JWT secret remains out of sync with `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`.
- Attempted resolution via Supabase MCP tool surface: confirmed no tool exists to rotate the project JWT secret. The fix path is a human-performed dashboard action (Project Settings → API → JWT Settings → set secret to the MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET value, OR rotate MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET via `supabase secrets set` to match the dashboard's current secret). Neither path is executable from within Ralph.
- No product code changed. `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` + `src/lib/time.ts` + the US-036 feat commit (`be06466`) remain the shipped implementation. The probe script is kept as a reusable one-command diagnostic so future iterations can re-check the drift state without writing fresh probe code.
- Files changed: `scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` (new), `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-036 notes updated with 3rd-iter re-probe verdict), `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp n/a (still blocked by upstream infra)
- Code-reviewer verdict: not invoked (no product changes — diagnostic tooling + notes only)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Run `bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` at the top of any iteration that plans to touch a UI screen doing direct PostgREST reads** (anything under `(creator)/` or `(lister)/` that calls `.from('x').select()` with the cached token). A 401 PGRST301 means the UI state is unverifiable via mobile-mcp regardless of story scope — log a notes entry and advance. A 200 means we can proceed as normal. Same probe also answers the question "is this story's mobile-mcp step viable" in one shot, saving a full signup-and-launch cycle.
  - **`chore(ralph)` commit pattern is appropriate for meta-iterations that produce only notes + diagnostic tooling.** Precedent: commits `5a937a5` (US-033 mark-complete), `a2f0416` (US-031 mark-complete), `b8235e5` (progress-aware watchdog). When a story's code is already committed (as US-036's was in `be06466`) but the iteration can't advance `passes:true` due to upstream env blockers, the chore commit captures the iteration's verification evidence without pretending the story completed.
  - **The JWT-drift blocker affects stories that hit PostgREST directly; it does NOT affect stories that route reads through edge functions.** Advance strategy: prefer stories whose data path is `app → edge-function (custom JWT verify) → service-role DB`. US-041 (`apply-to-listing` edge function), US-042 (Apply modal — calls US-041), and most lister-side creation flows (US-049+) follow this shape. Feed + profile + direct-PostgREST-list screens stay blocked until the dashboard fix lands.
